name: South London Gallery
description: Free contemporary art exhibitions, artist talks, film screenings, performances, supper clubs, zine fairs. Open late Wednesdays until 9pm.

name: Autograph
description: Free photography exhibitions exploring race, identity, representation and social justice. Based in Hackney since 1988.

name: Whitechapel Gallery
description: Major contemporary art gallery. Free exhibitions on Thursday nights until 9pm. First Thursdays celebrations with curated East London art recommendations.

name: ICA (Institute of Contemporary Arts)
description: Contemporary arts centre on The Mall. Open until 11pm Tue-Sun. Film, exhibitions, talks, music and performances.

name: Barbican Centre
description: Major arts centre with gallery, cinema, theatre, concert hall. Gallery open late Thu-Sat until 8pm. Free foyer exhibitions and events.

name: The Castle Cinema
description: Independent crowdfunded community cinema in Hackney. Pay What You Can screenings. Member-shaped programming.

name: The Lexi Cinema
description: Volunteer-run independent cinema in Kensal Rise. 100% distributable profits go to charity.

name: Rio Cinema
description: London's oldest community-run cinema in Dalston. Film festivals including Kurdish Film Festival, Fringe! Queer Film & Arts Fest, Doc'n Roll.

name: Genesis Cinema
description: Cultural hub in Whitechapel. Affordable films plus live music, poetry nights, swing events, festivals and community programming.

name: The Garden Cinema
description: Intimate two-screen cinema in Covent Garden. Bi-weekly free screenings for members. Bar events.

name: Rich Mix
description: Community arts space in Shoreditch. Three cinema screens, spoken word, theatre, live music. Three bars.

name: ArtHouse Crouch End
description: Cinema, art, live music, theatre, dance, workshops, comedy. Community-focused arts venue. Tickets from £7.

name: The Phoenix Cinema
description: London's longest-running cinema, open since 1910. Independent not-for-profit single-screen cinema owned by charitable trust.

name: Prince Charles Cinema
description: Cult cinema in Leicester Square. Movie marathons, £2.50 member tickets, sing-a-long screenings, event nights.

name: BFI Southbank
description: Four screens showing classic, independent and world cinema. Free BFI Mediatheque with hundreds of archive titles.

name: Gresham College
description: Over 100 free public lectures per year on astronomy, medicine, history, politics, law and more. Based in Holborn.

name: Conway Hall
description: Home of the Ethical Society. Free speech platform with London Thinks debates, talks on social, political and cultural topics.

name: LSE Public Events
description: Free public lecture programme with podcasts. Searchable by topic and speaker. Open to all.

name: Royal Institute of Philosophy
description: Free public annual lecture series. Centenary series 2025/26: Philosophy in Retrospect and Prospect.

name: Intelligence Squared
description: Debates, talks and discussions in politics, economics, history, science and culture. Venues include Union Chapel and Barbican.

name: How to Academy
description: Talks from thinkers, artists, scientists, entrepreneurs. Venues include Art Workers Guild in Bloomsbury and Barbican Hall.

name: London Review Bookshop
description: Author talks, readings, film screenings, debates about literature, history and politics. Events twice weekly.

name: Foyles
description: Major independent bookshop on Charing Cross Road. Regular author conversations, panel discussions, literary talks.

name: Lala Books
description: Independent bookshop hosting poetry nights, author readings, literary events. Rooted in local community.

name: Burley Fisher Books
description: Independent bookshop in Dalston. Finalist for Independent Bookshop of the Year 2026. Regular events.

name: Backstory Books
description: Independent bookshop in Balham. Three-time consecutive finalist for Bookshop of the Year. Author events and discussions.

name: Bàrd Books
description: Independent bookshop on Roman Road. Two-time shortlisted for Bookshop of the Year.

name: BookBar
description: Bookshop-bar with outposts in Chelsea and Islington. Finalist for Independent Bookshop of the Year 2026.

name: The Albany
description: SE London's leading arts centre in Deptford. Community collaboration, artist development. Youth takeovers, over-65s arts club.

name: Bernie Grant Arts Centre
description: £15m multi-arts centre in Tottenham. 274-seat auditorium, studio, café/bar, enterprise centre.

name: Battersea Arts Centre
description: Home for radical theatre, workspace. Near Clapham Junction. Known for innovative community programming.

name: Old Diorama Arts Centre
description: Euston's centre for artistic creation and community action. Home to creative organisations and artists.

name: The Space
description: Performing arts centre in a converted church on the Isle of Dogs. Arts, events and workshops.

name: Second Wave Youth Arts
description: Free youth arts programmes for ages 11-25 in Deptford. Drama, songwriting, music, film, photography.

name: Brixton House
description: Formerly Ovalhouse Theatre. Theatre, dance, live art in Brixton. Focus on new work and diverse voices.

name: London Hackspace
description: Non-profit community-run workshop. 3D printing, laser cutting, electronics, metalwork. Open Wednesday evenings.

name: South London Makerspace
description: Community workshop in Herne Hill. Laser cutter, screenprinting, 3D printing, ceramics. Member-run, volunteer basis.

name: Makerversity
description: Maker community in Somerset House. Wednesday breakfasts, members bar, discounted exhibitions and events.

name: Blackhorse Workshop
description: Public resource workshop for making, mending and DIY. Space for the creative community with tools and technical support.

name: London Sculpture Workshop
description: London's first open access sculpture workshop. Metal, wood, mould-making, ceramics. Not-for-profit community interest company.

name: Apples and Snakes (Jawdance)
description: London's warmest spoken word and open mic night. Free to attend. Supportive space for all voices.

name: Hidden Literature (MY WORD!)
description: Open mic night at Grow Hackney. Spoken word, poetry, hip-hop, R&B, neo-soul. Welcoming to first-timers.

name: Grow Hackney
description: Community venue hosting poetry, music, and cultural events in Hackney.

name: Green Note
description: Award-winning intimate live music venue and vegetarian café-bar in Camden. Folk, Americana, blues, jazz, world music every night.

name: Cafe Oto
description: Experimental and avant-garde music in Dalston. Free jazz, improvisation, sound art. The weirdest and most creative sounds in London.

name: The Lexington
description: Bastion of independent music between King's Cross and Angel since 2008. Gigs, pop quizzes, events.

name: Union Chapel
description: Working church and stunningly beautiful live music venue in Highbury & Islington.

name: The Bull's Head (Barnes)
description: Live music seven days a week for over 60 years. Iconic Jazz Room in Barnes.

name: Moth Club
description: Former servicemen's club in Hackney. Intimate gigs. Secret shows from Lady Gaga, Foo Fighters and more.

name: The Cockpit
description: Small flexible fringe venue in Marylebone. Music, comedy, dance, and theatre.

name: Bread and Roses Theatre
description: Consistent powerhouse of the South London fringe. Under 100 seats. New work focus.

name: Hen and Chickens Theatre
description: Jewel of North London fringe. Pub theatre above a bar. Home to Unrestricted View.

name: Lion and Unicorn Theatre
description: Black-box space above a Kentish Town pub. Daring, experimental work. Run by Proforca Theatre Company.

name: The Finborough Theatre
description: Pioneering West London fringe theatre. Known for discovering new talent. Running since 1980.

name: Pleasance Theatre
description: One of the most exciting fringe theatres in London since 1995. Two spaces: Mainhouse (230) and Stagespace (54).

name: Blue Elephant Theatre
description: Fully wheelchair accessible fringe theatre in Camberwell.

name: Half Moon Theatre
description: UK's leading small-scale young people's venue and touring company. Based in Limehouse, East London.

name: Greenwich Theatre
description: Theatre in Greenwich, near the DLR and mainline station. Mix of professional and community programming.

name: Tate Modern
description: Free contemporary art. Tate Lates: art, music, film, talks and workshops until 10pm on select Fridays.

name: Science Museum Lates
description: Last Wednesday of each month, 6:30-10pm. Themed nights with interactive activities, quizzes, silent discos, experiments.

name: National Portrait Gallery
description: Open late Fri-Sat until 9pm. Artist-led drawing classes, jazz nights, story tours.

name: The Old Operating Theatre Museum
description: Film screenings, secret concerts, quirky historical talks, Victorian surgical demonstrations. In a church attic.

name: Mam Sham Supper Club
description: Comedy-meets-culinary supper club. Three-course menus inspired by three comedy acts. Seats up to 200.

name: Khao Suppers
description: Monthly Gujarati supper club. Started in an East London home. Four-course menus of classic Gujarati dishes.

name: Dinner Ladies
description: Sustainable supper clubs with Asian flex. Themed events including Wasted Supper — turning unwanted leftovers into delicious meals.

name: Eleven98
description: Hackney-sourced supper club. All produce from local area. Sustainability-first dinners.

name: Wimbledon BookFest
description: London's leading literary festival. 15-25 October 2026. Year-round education programme. Partnership with Lahore Literary Festival.

name: The RSA
description: Royal Society of Arts. Free public events, talks, and lectures on social change, innovation and creativity.

name: Peckham Festival
description: Free long weekend of art, performances, activities. Made in Peckham open studios. Art trail through warehouses and streets.

name: London Gallery Weekend
description: Free annual event with 130+ contemporary galleries. Talks, family activities, special events across London.

name: Camden Fringe
description: Annual fringe festival in August. Alternative to Edinburgh. Cheap tickets, new material in intimate venues.

name: Ronnie Scott's
description: Spiritual home of British jazz since 1959. Seven days a week, often two shows a night.

name: Landmark Arts Centre
description: Art exhibitions, workshops and live events in Teddington, SW London.

name: Brady Arts & Community Centre
description: Multi-purpose venue in Whitechapel near Brick Lane. Performances, exhibitions, rehearsals, workshops.

name: Outernet London
description: Free immersive experience at Tottenham Court Road. Part gallery, part digital art. Open until midnight some days.

name: Eatwith London (formerly Grub Club)
description: Platform for supper clubs and alternative dining experiences with local chefs.

name: Tate Britain
description: Free British art collection. After-hours events every couple of months with sound, visuals, activities and discussions.

name: Natural History Museum
description: Free museum with late night events including legendary silent disco starting at 10pm.

name: ANNKA KULTYS Gallery
description: Contemporary and digital art gallery on Hackney Road. Wheelchair accessible.

name: Oriole Bar
description: Live jazz, blues, and world music Wed-Sat in Covent Garden.

name: Boisdale
description: Jazz, soul and blues in Canary Wharf and Belgravia. Sunday blues brunch, Saturday soul lunches, live bands.
